review: Tucson staple. My dad took me here when I was, like, seventeen years old and made me order the "cabeza tacos." After I ate the tacos, which were stuffed with the most tender, melt in your mouth, delightful meat I have ever tasted, he broke the news that I had eaten "a beef head taco." Thanks, Dad (but it was delicious). Nowadays I don't really eat cow cheeks, although I do recommend anything "cabeza" to my carnivore friends and family. Now I just get bean burritos. Sounds boring, but at BK the bean burritos are OUT OF THIS WORLD! I just don't understand the prices. The burrito I got was pretty tiny and it was seven bucks. But worth it.
